channel.title: Math Learning Site – Worksheets
channel.description: Recent content in Worksheets on Math Learning Site

item.title: One-Step Equations Worksheet
item.description:
This one-step equations worksheet is designed to help students master the fundamental building blocks of algebra. By focusing on a single operation at a time, learners can build the confidence and speed necessary for more complex math. Whether you need practice with addition, subtraction, multiplication, or division, our randomized generator provides unlimited high-quality practice for the classroom or home study.

Intro: Master the Basics of Algebra

This one-step equations worksheet gives students targeted algebra practice with simple linear equations that can be solved in one move. It is a strong starting point for learning how equations work, and it helps build the habits students need before moving into two-step and multi-step equations.

What is a One-Step Equation?

A one-step equation is an algebraic equation that requires only one mathematical move to find the value of the variable, usually represented by letters like xxx or yyy.

Think of an equation like a balanced scale. To solve it, your goal is to isolate the variable, which means getting the letter by itself on one side of the equals sign. Because these problems only require one step or operation to solve, they are the perfect starting point for anyone learning linear equations.

How to Solve One-Step Equations

To solve these problems, you use inverse operations. An inverse operation is simply the opposite of the operation currently being applied to the variable.

The 3 Golden Rules

  1. Identify the operation. Look at what is happening to the variable. Is it being added, subtracted, multiplied, or divided?
  2. Use the inverse. Perform the opposite operation to undo it.
  3. Stay balanced. Whatever you do to one side of the equation, you must do to the other side.

Example Walkthrough

Problem: Solve x+8=15x + 8 = 15x+8=15

Step 1: The variable xxx is having 888 added to it.

Step 2: The inverse of addition is subtraction. Subtract 888 from both sides.

Step 3: x+8−8=15−8x + 8 - 8 = 15 - 8x+8−8=15−8

Solution: x=7x = 7x=7

One-Step Equations Worksheet

12 questions Page 1 of 2

Solve each problem. Show your work.

Name:
Date:

One-Step Equations Worksheet Answer Key

12 questions Page 2 of 2

Check your work using the correct answers below.

FAQ

Q: Why are these called “one-step” equations?
A: They are called one-step equations because you only need to perform a single mathematical operation to isolate the variable and solve the problem.

Q: What are the four inverse operations?
A: Addition and subtraction are inverses of each other, and multiplication and division are inverses of each other.

item.title: Two-Step Equations Worksheet
item.description:
This two-step equations worksheet helps students practice undoing two operations in the correct order. The worksheet generates 12 randomized questions for printable practice, followed by an answer key on page 2.

Two-Step Equations Worksheet

12 questions Page 1 of 2

Solve each problem. Show your work.

Name:
Date:

Two-Step Equations Worksheet Answer Key

12 questions Page 2 of 2

Check your work using the correct answers below.

item.title: Multi-Step Equations Worksheet
item.description:
This multi-step equations worksheet is designed for students who are ready to combine like terms, distribute, and solve longer linear equations. It includes 12 randomized printable questions with the answer key on page 2.

Multi-Step Equations Worksheet

12 questions Page 1 of 2

Solve each problem. Show your work.

Name:
Date:

Multi-Step Equations Worksheet Answer Key

12 questions Page 2 of 2

Check your work using the correct answers below.

item.title: Variables on Both Sides Worksheet
item.description:
This variables-on-both-sides worksheet gives students practice collecting variable terms and constants before solving. Each printable set includes 12 randomized questions, and the answer key appears on page 2.

Variables on Both Sides Worksheet

12 questions Page 1 of 2

Solve each problem. Show your work.

Name:
Date:

Variables on Both Sides Worksheet Answer Key

12 questions Page 2 of 2

Check your work using the correct answers below.

item.title: Mixed Linear Equations Worksheet
item.description:
This mixed linear equations worksheet brings together one-step, two-step, multi-step, and variables-on-both-sides practice in one printable review. It includes 16 randomized questions for broader coverage, with the answer key on page 2.

Mixed Linear Equations Worksheet

16 questions Page 1 of 2

Solve each problem. Show your work.

Name:
Date:

Mixed Linear Equations Worksheet Answer Key

16 questions Page 2 of 2

Check your work using the correct answers below.

item.title: Graphing Linear Equations
item.description:
This graphing linear equations worksheet is interactive instead of print-first, because graph questions are clearer and more useful on screen. Each new set includes both styles students need while learning graphing: some questions show a graph and ask you to read it, while others give an equation and ask you to choose the correct graph.

What is graphing a linear equation?

Graphing a linear equation means showing all of the solutions to the equation on a coordinate plane. In Algebra I, students use slope, intercepts, special line types, and plotted points to connect equations with their visual graphs.

How to solve graphing questions

Start by deciding which direction the question is moving. If the graph is shown first, look for the slope, y-intercept, x-intercept, or points on the line. If the equation is shown first, identify its slope and intercept and then compare those features to the answer choices.

For example, the equation y=2x+1y = 2x + 1y=2x+1 should cross the y-axis at 111 and rise 222 for every 111 step right. A horizontal line like y=−3y=-3y=−3 stays flat, while a vertical line like x=2x=2x=2 goes straight up and down.

Interactive Worksheet

This activity includes:

  • graph-in-question prompts
  • graph-as-answer prompts
  • slope and intercept questions
  • matching equations to graphs
  • horizontal and vertical line cases

Graphing Linear Equations Worksheet

8 questions Interactive worksheet

Answer each question using the radio buttons. You will get feedback as soon as you choose an answer.

Name:
Date:

FAQ

Q: Why is this one interactive instead of printable?
A: Graphing questions are much easier to read and answer on screen, especially when answer choices are also graphs.

Q: Does this include both major graphing formats?
A: Yes. The set mixes questions where the graph appears in the prompt and questions where the graph appears in the answer choices.

item.title: Systems of Linear Equations Intro Worksheet
item.description:
This introductory systems of linear equations worksheet blends substitution, elimination, and beginner-friendly word-problem practice. The printable worksheet includes 12 randomized questions, followed by an answer key on page 2.

Systems of Linear Equations Intro Worksheet

12 questions Page 1 of 2

Solve each problem. Show your work.
